filter_pushdown.comment: DATE '1998-09-02' encoded as days_since_epoch = 10471 (1998-12-01 minus 90 days)

logical_plan.group_by_comment: l_returnflag (3 distinct: A,N,R) x l_linestatus (2 distinct: F,O) = 6 groups max — flat array indexed by (returnflag_code * 2 + linestatus_code)
logical_plan.late_materialization_reason: All needed columns are numeric (int32/int64) — no expensive string loads in hot path. Dictionary-encoded returnflag/linestatus decoded once to integer keys for grouping.

data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[6] per thread — struct { int64_t sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_discount; int64_t count; } agg[6]
data_structures.aggregation_comment: 6 groups fit in a single cache line cluster. No hash table needed. Index = returnflag_dict_id * 2 + linestatus_dict_id. Thread-local arrays merged in O(6*nthreads) = trivial.
data_structures.zone_map: mmap(indexes/zone_map_l_shipdate.bin) — 600 entries of {int32 min, int32 max, uint32 count}. Skip blocks where min > 10471 (tail pruning on ascending-sorted column).

parallelism.comment: Block size matches morsel size (100K rows/block). Each thread pulls morsels via atomic fetch_add on block counter. 600 blocks / 64 threads ≈ 9-10 blocks per thread.

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t count} x 600 (12 bytes/entry)
index_usage.full_pass_condition: block.max <= 10471 => no per-row check needed, process all rows
index_usage.estimated_blocks_skipped: ~3-5 tail blocks out of 600 (data sorted ascending, cutoff near max date)

physical_plan.scan_comment: All 7 columns loaded per morsel. Fused scan+filter+aggregate in one tight loop. No intermediate row materialization. SIMD AVX2 comparison on l_shipdate for filter.
physical_plan.memory_comment: All columns are contiguous binary arrays. Sequential access pattern maximizes hardware prefetcher effectiveness. 7 columns * 100K rows * avg 6 bytes = ~4.2MB per morsel, fits in L3 cache (44MB / 64 threads = 687KB per thread — fits in L3 with prefetch overlap).

simd.comment: Process 8 int32 shipdates per AVX2 instruction. Produces 256-bit mask of qualifying rows. Branchless accumulation into SIMD partial-sum registers.

decimal_handling.arithmetic: integer arithmetic on int64_t throughout — defer division to final output only
decimal_handling.disc_price: l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) where discount stored as scale=2 integer (e.g., 0.07 stored as 7)
decimal_handling.charge: disc_price * (100 + l_tax) — all integer multiply, divide by scale at output

final_sort.comment: At most 6 result rows. Any sort method is O(1) effectively.

estimated_cost.effective_bytes_after_zone_prune: ~2.8 GB (~99% blocks pass, only tail blocks skipped)
estimated_cost.rationale: Current GenDB time is 108ms. With 64 threads on morsel-driven scan, flat-array aggregation (0 hash overhead), AVX2 filter, and zone map pruning, we eliminate hash table build cost entirely and reduce aggregation overhead to near-zero. Target is competitive with Umbra/DuckDB at ~83ms. Dominant cost is memory bandwidth: ~2.8GB / (64 threads * ~3GB/s per thread effective) ≈ 70-90ms wall time.
